title: Train a multi-horizon hazard tower from exact future pit distance
summary: Survival/event-time work shows that discretizing time-to-event into multiple binary tasks is often stronger than a single next-step label.
rationale: Survival/event-time work shows that discretizing time-to-event into multiple binary tasks is often stronger than a single next-step label. Here, the exact next pit distance is available in training, so `PitWithin1/2/3/5` or a discrete hazard target can smooth supervision and teach the model the shape of the pit window.
implementation_hint: Create future-distance labels within each driver-race sequence, train either one multi-output model or several tied models for `within_k` targets, and convert outputs back to a next-lap probability or use them as stacker features. Enforce causal construction and ignore censored tail rows after the final observed pit if needed.
expected_effect: Usually helps borderline rows near the pit window and reduces brittleness from treating `t+1` positives and `t+2` near-misses as equally unrelated.
risk: If auxiliary horizons are weighted poorly, the model may optimize broader pit-window detection instead of the exact next-lap ranking needed for AUC.
